content:
# Agentic Retrieval Protocol — Design Issue Created (2026-03-27)

## What
Issue #47: Define how AI agents should interact with corvia as a behavioral protocol, not just tool interfaces. Five design areas: adaptive retrieval, context injection for subagents, knowledge gap signaling, retrieval feedback loops, and write discipline.

## Why
Current agent-corvia interaction is rule-based ("always call corvia first") not intelligent. Three key gaps:
1. No result quality assessment or retry — agent moves on to Grep/Glob if corvia returns low-relevance results
2. Subagents (reviewers, implementers, explorers) have no MCP access — they work blind to organizational knowledge
3. No feedback loop — corvia never learns which queries go unanswered or which results were stale

## Key Insight
Corvia's retrieval pipeline (how results are ranked) is half the story. The interaction protocol (how agents query, assess, retry, propagate, and contribute) is the other half. This is what distinguishes a memory system from a search engine.

## Relationship to Other Work
- Access tracking (issue #12) captures "was accessed" — this extends to "was useful"
- Tiered knowledge decay uses access signals — quality signals would improve tier transitions
- BM25 hybrid search (#37) improves retrieval quality — this improves retrieval usage
- Dev-loop skill benefits but this is broader — applies to ALL agent interactions
